title: Goroutine Pools, Context Causes, Backpressure, and Shutdown · Language Lens: Channel Ownership
summary: A goroutine is launched per URL, workers close shared channels, cancellation stops fetch but not parsing or writes, and main returns before cleanup. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go channel ownership increment in bounded leak-free Go crawl pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Assign channel creation, senders, receiver, closer, buffering rationale, and cancellation behavior without double close or blocked send.
Bound frontier, workers, open bodies, parser memory, report buffers, files, and browser-service requests with end-to-end backpressure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Channel Ownership model · Theory Channel Ownership, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Any goroutine that notices completion may close a shared channel.
